  "findings": [
    "Periodic, rhythmic impact-like clicking sound indicating a localized defect on a bearing race or rolling element",
    "Low-frequency repetition rate consistent with a slow-turning conveyor drive shaft",
    "Underlying machine bed noise is relatively clean, isolating the fault to the bearing internals"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Schedule immediate bearing replacement during the next planned maintenance window",
    "Monitor bearing temperature and vibration levels in the interim",
    "Inspect lubrication for metal particulate contamination"
  ],
  "rationale": "The audio recording reveals a distinct, rhythmic, low-frequency impact sound (clicking/tapping) superimposed on a quiet background. This is characteristic of a localized defect (such as spalling or a crack) on the bearing's inner race, outer race, or a rolling element passing through the load zone at a constant slow speed. The bearing is degraded and requires replacement to prevent catastrophic failure.",
  "references": [
    "ISO 13373 (Condition monitoring and diagnostics of machines)",
    "SKF Bearing Damage and Failure Analysis Guide"
  ]
}
